title: Design System Documentation Standard (DSDS) v0.1
description: A standard, machine-readable format for design system documentation covering components, design tokens, token groups, themes, styles, and patterns. A DSDS document contains one or more documentation groups — named collections of typed entities. Each entity carries a `kind` discriminator that identifies it as a component, token, token-group, theme, style, or pattern. Entity schemas are defined in entities/. Guideline schemas are defined in guidelines/. Common schemas are defined in common/.

$schema.description: URI reference to the DSDS JSON Schema for validation.

dsdsVersion.description: The version of the DSDS specification this document conforms to.

purpose.description: The purpose of the design system as a whole — why it exists, what problems it solves, who it serves, and when teams should or should not adopt it. Helps stakeholders, new team members, and external consumers understand the system's intent before exploring individual components or tokens.

description.description: A high-level description of the design system's purpose and the problems it solves.

useCases.description: Scenario-driven guidance describing when this design system is or is not the right choice. Positive cases describe situations where the system should be adopted. Negative cases describe situations where a different approach is more appropriate, with an explanation of why.

bestPractices.description: System-level best practices that apply across the entire design system — high-altitude guidance for teams adopting, contributing to, or consuming the system. These are not component-specific rules (those live in each entity's guidelines array) but cross-cutting principles like 'always use semantic tokens instead of raw values', 'test all components at 200% zoom', or 'do not modify token values at the component level'. Each entry is a self-contained rule paired with a rationale.

documentation.description: One or more documentation groups. Each group is a named collection of typed entities — components, tokens, token groups, themes, styles, and/or patterns. Multiple groups allow a single DSDS file to organize artifacts into logical sections (e.g., one group for foundations, another for components).

documentationGroup.description: A named collection of design system documentation entities. Contains a single `items` array of typed entities — components, tokens, token groups, themes, styles, and/or patterns mixed freely in any order. Each entity is identified by its `kind` discriminator property. At least one entity must be present.

name.description: Human-readable name of the collection (e.g., 'Acme Design System', 'Color Tokens', 'Button Documentation').

items.description: The entities in this documentation group. Each item is a typed entity — its `kind` property determines which schema applies. Entities of different types can be mixed freely in the same array. The ordering is significant: tools SHOULD preserve it for display. Authors MAY organize entities in whatever order best serves their documentation (e.g., tokens before components, or grouped by domain).
